data.title: Veracyte, Inc. 2025 Q1 - Results - Earnings Call Presentation
data.description: The following slide deck was published by Veracyte, Inc. in conjunction with their 2025 Q1 earnings call.

data.title: Fidelity Limited Term Municipal Income Fund Q1 2025 Commentary
data.description:
Investment Approach

  • Fidelity® Limited Term Municipal Income Fund is a diversified national municipal bond strategy investing in general obligation and revenue- or tax-backed municipal securities across the short-intermediate part of the yield curve.
  • Our investment approach focuses on fundamental credit analysis, yield-curve positioning and an analysis of the structural characteristics of each security.
  • The fund's interest rate sensitivity is targeted closely to that of its benchmark to prevent interest rate speculation from overwhelming research-based strategies that we deem to have a higher likelihood of long-term success.
  • We emphasize a total-return approach that seeks to generate a high level of tax-exempt income, consistent with the preservation of capital.

[2] This expense ratio is from the most recent prospectus and generally is based on amounts incurred during the most recent fiscal year, or estimated amounts for the current fiscal year in the case of a newly launched fund. It does not include any fee waivers or reimbursements, which would be reflected in the fund's net expense ratio.

Past performance is no guarantee of future results. Investment return and principal value of an investment will fluctuate; therefore, you may have a gain or loss when you sell your shares. Current performance may be higher or lower than the performance stated. Performance shown is that of the fund's Retail Class shares (if multiclass). You may own another share class of the fund with a different expense structure and, thus, have different returns. To learn more or to obtain the most recent month-end or other share-class performance, visit fidelity.com/performance, institutional.fidelity.com, or 401k.com. Total returns are historical and include change in share value and reinvestment of dividends and capital gains, if any.

Cumulative total returns are reported as of the period indicated.

For definitions and other important information, please see the Definitions and Important Information section of this Fund Review.

Municipal Market Review

Heavy new-issue supply and a sharp decline in investor demand in March crimped returns for investment-grade tax-exempt municipal bonds for the first quarter of 2025. The Bloomberg Municipal Bond Index returned -0.22% (including interest payments and price changes) for the three months. Muni bonds, however, continued to be supported by a steady credit environment and strong overall economic data.

The asset class began 2025 on a modestly positive note, with the index gaining 0.50% in January. Muni bond yields – which move in the opposite direction of prices – rose early in the month when a better-than-expected employment report prompted investors to push out their expectations for Fed rate cuts. But yields declined by month end in response to tame inflation reports and solid investor demand for munis. Elevated new-issue supply was a headwind for the muni market in January, with the amount of new bonds issued outpacing reinvestment income from maturities, calls and coupons. Even so, demand remained firm as investors looked to lock in attractive tax-exempt yields.

Munis rose 0.99% in February amid significant market uncertainty about fiscal policy under the new Trump administration. Bond yields declined as rate-cut expectations increased, partly due to concern that federal layoffs and spending cuts might drag down economic growth. This triggered a U.S. equity market sell-off that sent investors seeking haven in high-quality fixed-income assets, including munis. Municipal supply remained robust and demand was solid.

Munis then returned -1.69% in March, a typically difficult month for the asset class. Sales of new muni debt remained elevated, investor demand declined significantly, and reinvestment faded as fewer bonds matured compared with January and February. Additionally, some investors sold munis to pay their tax bills due in April, creating another headwind.

Full-quarter returns showed lackluster results along the maturity spectrum, with one-, three- and five-year muni securities (+1.05%, +1.01% and +0.91%, respectively) faring best and 15-, 20-, and 22-plus-year securities (-0.60%, -1.29% and -1.46%, respectively) bringing up the rear. From a sector perspective, municipal revenue bonds performed roughly in line with general obligation bonds. Among GOs, state-backed bonds (-0.00%) outpaced local government-backed debt (-0.50%).

In the revenue bond category, the corporate-backed IDR/PCR sector (+0.91%) and the longer-duration housing sector (+0.78%) outpaced weightier index segments, including special tax (-0.67%), hospitals (-0.55%) and education (-0.38%). On a total return basis, lower-quality investment-grade bonds (rated A and BAA – the Bloomberg equivalent to BBB – respectively) modestly outperformed higher-rated debt.

Muni tax-backed credit fundamentals remained solid throughout the quarter, and the risk of credit-rating downgrades appeared low for most government issuers.

Performance Review

Detailed Fund Attribution Relative To Benchmark

Strategy: Sector Allocation

Market Environment

Sector performance was mixed, but revenue bonds modestly outperformed general obligation bonds, mainly due to their higher yields.

Fund Positioning

(Impact vs. Benchmark)

The fund's overweights in housing, health care and airports contributed to performance versus the Bloomberg 1-6 Year Municipal Bond Index. (Positive)

Strategy: Credit

Market Environment

Credit yield spreads tightened modestly across rating categories.

Fund Positioning

(Impact vs. Benchmark)
  • A higher average yield on the fund's underlying holdings helped it gain more carry than the index. (Positive)
  • Overweights in bonds backed by Love Field Airport (Dallas) and by John F. Kennedy International Airport (New York City) also boosted the relative result. (Positive)
  • Conversely, underweights in bonds backed by the state of California and by airports in the state of Washington detracted. (Negative)

Strategy: Yield Curve

Market Environment

The muni yield curve steepened as shorter-maturity yields fell and longer-maturity yields rose.

Fund Positioning

(Impact vs. Benchmark)

The fund's yield-curve positioning modestly detracted from relative performance. (Negative)

Strategy: Additional Factors

Market Environment

The fund and its benchmark employ somewhat different methodologies in estimating the prices of municipal securities, most of which trade infrequently.

Fund Positioning

(Impact vs. Benchmark)

Pricing methodology differences detracted. (Negative)

Outlook and Positioning

Going forward, the political landscape will continue to be intertwined with the larger macroeconomic environment, including fiscal spending and monetary policy, the factors that have historically dominated muni bond performance.

In our view, uncertainty regarding the path of economic growth, unemployment, inflation and tariffs under the Trump administration supports restraint around the outlook for additional policy rate cuts in 2025. Furthermore, Congressional renegotiation of expiring tax provisions in the 2017 Tax Cuts and Jobs Act has the potential to impact the tax-exempt market.

The expectation of rising municipal governmental expenses in the face of more limited tax revenue growth and depletion of federal aid and grants may heighten the need for municipal issuers to exercise budgetary discipline. In addition, state and local elections will influence the trajectory for tax rates and, ultimately, the value of municipal bonds within those localities.

In Q1, tax-backed municipal issuer finances remained mostly solid. Though growth of tax receipts may continue to slow as they have over the past year, we believe most state and local governments are

well-positioned to adjust. Most states, for instance, have prudently managed recent surpluses by increasing their rainy-day reserves, paying down debt and strengthening pension plan funding.

It is important for investors to remember that Fidelity's municipal bond portfolios are constructed with a careful and intentional emphasis on issue selection, especially with consideration to the liquidity of the security and the financial resiliency of its issuer. In the current environment, we continue to evaluate each fund holding and are monitoring those that may be more financially challenged than others.

Going forward, and as always, we will seek to identify opportunities to purchase municipal securities that offer both attractive valuations and a solid risk/reward profile. At quarter end, we continue to invest for strong risk-adjusted returns while remaining cautious with yield-curve positioning, given the likelihood of interest-rate volatility.

We remain committed to the approach of building individual exposures in the portfolio that reflect risks with which we are comfortable, at entry prices we believe offer strong relative value.

FUND INFORMATION

Manager(s): Team Managed

Trading Symbol: FSTFX

Start Date: December 24, 1986

Size (in millions): $2,637.50

Morningstar Category: Fund Muni National Short

The municipal market can be affected by adverse tax, legislative, or political changes and the financial condition of the issuers of municipal securities. Fixed income investments entail interest rate risk (as interest rates rise bond prices usually fall), the risk of issuer default, issuer credit risk and inflation risk.

Income exempt from federal income tax may be subject to state or local tax. All or a portion of the fund's income may be subject to the federal alternative minimum tax. Income or fund distributions attributable to capital gains are usually subject to both state and federal income taxes. Leverage can increase market exposure and magnify investment risk.

Definitions and Important Information

Unless otherwise expressly disclosed to you in writing, the information provided in this material is for educational purposes only. Any viewpoints expressed by Fidelity are not intended to be used as a primary basis for your investment decisions and are based on facts and circumstances at the point in time they are made and are not particular to you.

Accordingly, nothing in this material constitutes impartial investment advice or advice in a fiduciary capacity, as defined or under the Employee Retirement Income Security Act of 1974 or the Internal Revenue Code of 1986, both as amended. Fidelity and its representatives may have a conflict of interest in the products or services mentioned in this material because they have a financial interest in the products or services and may receive compensation, directly or indirectly, in connection with the management, distribution, and/or servicing of these products or services, including Fidelity funds, certain third-party funds and products, and certain investment services. Before making any investment decisions, you should take into account all of the particular facts and circumstances of your or your client's individual situation and reach out to a professional adviser, if applicable.

CHARACTERISTICS

Duration is a measure of a security's price sensitivity to changes in interest rates. Duration differs from maturity in that it considers a security's interest payments in addition to the amount of time until the security reaches maturity, and also takes into account certain maturity shortening features (e.g., demand features, interest rate resets, and call options) when applicable. Securities with longer durations generally tend to be more sensitive to interest rate changes than securities with shorter durations. A fund with a longer average duration generally can be expected to be more sensitive to interest rate changes than a fund with a shorter average duration.

30-day SEC Yield is a standard yield calculation developed by the Securities and Exchange Commission for bond funds. The yield is calculated by dividing the net investment income per share earned during the 30-day period by the maximum offering price per share on the last day of the period. The yield figure reflects the dividends and interest earned during the 30-day period, after the deduction of the fund's expenses. It is sometimes referred to as "SEC 30-Day Yield" or "standardized yield".

30-Day SEC Restated Yield is the fund's 30-day yield without applicable waivers or reimbursements, stated as of month-end.

30-day SEC Tax-Equivalent Yield shows what you would have to earn on a taxable investment to equal the fund's tax-free yield, if you are in the 37% effective federal income tax bracket and also subject to the 3.8% Medicare Contribution tax, but does not reflect the payment of the federal alternative minimum tax, if applicable. Medicare Contribution tax is a tax on non-municipal investment income that applies to individuals with incomes over $200,000 (or $250,000, filing jointly). For state-specific funds, TEY is based not only on the highest federal tax rate (40.8%) but also the highest state tax rate. For state-specific funds, TEYs assume investors are state residents and would not be able to take an itemized deduction on their federal returns for state taxes on investment income. For NY funds, TEYs do not reflect the NY state tax rate that applies to income in excess of $5 million. For MD funds, TEYs reflect the highest city/county tax rates in MD and treat them the same as state taxes. Consult a tax professional for further detail.

Net Asset Value is the dollar value of one share of a fund; determined by taking the total assets of a fund, subtracting the total liabilities, and dividing by the total number of shares outstanding.

Subject to Alternative Minimum Tax is the percent of interest income subject to the alternative minimum tax (AMT) based on total net assets within a portfolio.

IMPORTANT FUND INFORMATION

Relative positioning data presented in this commentary is based on the fund's primary benchmark (index) unless a secondary benchmark is provided to assess performance.

INDICES

It is not possible to invest directly in an index. All indices represented are unmanaged. All indices include reinvestment of dividends and interest income unless otherwise noted.

Bloomberg 1-6 Year Municipal Bond Index is a market-value-weighted index of investment-grade fixed-rate municipal bonds with maturities between one and six years.

Bloomberg Municipal Bond Index is a market value-weighted index of investment-grade municipal bonds with maturities of one year or more.

LIPPER INFORMATION

Lipper Averages are averages of the performance of all mutual funds with their respective investment classification category. The number of funds in each category periodically changes. Lipper, a Refinitiv company, is a nationally recognized organization that ranks the performance of mutual funds.

MORNINGSTAR INFORMATION

© 2025 Morningstar, Inc. All rights reserved. The Morningstar information contained herein: (1) is proprietary to Morningstar and/or its content providers; (2) may not be copied or redistributed; and (3) is not warranted to be accurate, complete or timely. Neither Morningstar nor its content providers are responsible for any damages or losses arising from any use of this information. Fidelity does not review the Morningstar data and, for mutual fund performance, you should check the fund's current prospectus for the most up-to-date information concerning applicable loads, fees and expenses.

SECTOR WEIGHTS

Sector weights illustrate examples of market segments in which the fund may invest, and may not be representative of the fund's current or future investments. They should not be construed or used as a recommendation for any subset of the market.

Before investing in any mutual fund, please carefully consider the investment objectives, risks, charges, and expenses. For this and other information, call or write Fidelity for a free prospectus or, if available, a summary prospectus. Read it carefully before you invest.

Past performance is no guarantee of future results.

Views expressed are through the end of the period stated and do not necessarily represent the views of Fidelity. Views are subject to change at any time based upon market or other conditions and Fidelity disclaims any responsibility to update such views. These views may not be relied on as investment advice and, because investment decisions for a Fidelity fund are based on numerous factors, may not be relied on as an indication of trading intent on behalf of any Fidelity fund. The securities mentioned are not necessarily holdings invested in by the portfolio manager(s) or FMR LLC. References to specific company securities should not be construed as recommendations or investment advice.

Diversification does not ensure a profit or guarantee against a loss.

S&P 500 is a registered service mark of Standard & Poor's Financial Services LLC.

Other third-party marks appearing herein are the property of their respective owners.

All other marks appearing herein are registered or unregistered trademarks or service marks of FMR LLC or an affiliated company.

Fidelity Brokerage Services LLC, Member NYSE, SIPC, 900 Salem Street, Smithfield, RI 02917.

Fidelity Distributors Company LLC, 500 Salem Street, Smithfield, RI 02917.

© 2025 FMR LLC. All rights reserved.

Net Other Assets can include fund receivables, fund payables, and offsets to other derivative positions, as well as certain assets that do not fall into any of the portfolio composition categories. Depending on the extent to which the fund invests in derivatives and the number of positions that are held for future settlement, Net Other Assets can be a negative number.

Credit ratings for a rated issuer or security are categorized using the highest credit rating among the following three Nationally Recognized Statistical Rating Organizations ("NRSRO"): Moody's Investors Service (Moody's); Standard & Poor's Rating Services (S&P); or Fitch, Inc. Securities that are not rated by any of these three NRSRO's (e.g. equity securities) are categorized as Not Rated. All U.S. government securities are included in the U.S. Government category. The table information is based on the combined investments of the fund and its pro-rata share of any investments in other Fidelity funds.

It is sometimes referred to as "SEC 30-Day Yield" or "standardized yield".

30-Day SEC Restated Yield is the fund's 30-day yield without applicable waivers or reimbursements, stated as of month-end.

30-day SEC Tax-Equivalent Yield shows what you would have to earn on a taxable investment to equal the fund's tax-free yield, if you are in the 37% effective federal income tax bracket and also subject to the 3.8% Medicare Contribution tax, but does not reflect the payment of the federal alternative minimum tax, if applicable. Medicare Contribution tax is a tax on non-municipal investment income that applies to individuals with incomes over $200,000 (or $250,000, filing jointly). For state-specific funds, TEY is based not only on the highest federal tax rate (40.8%) but also the highest state tax rate. For state-specific funds, TEYs assume investors are state residents and would not be able to take an itemized deduction on their federal returns for state taxes on investment income. For NY funds, TEYs do not reflect the NY state tax rate that applies to income in excess of $5 million. For MD funds, TEYs reflect the highest city/county tax rates in MD and treat them the same as state taxes. Consult a tax professional for further detail.

Net Asset Value is the dollar value of one share of a fund; determined by taking the total assets of a fund, subtracting the total liabilities, and dividing by the total number of shares outstanding.

Subject to Alternative Minimum Tax is the percent of interest income subject to the alternative minimum tax (AMT) based on total net assets within a portfolio.

IMPORTANT FUND INFORMATION

INDICES

It is not possible to invest directly in an index. All indices represented are unmanaged. All indices include reinvestment of dividends and interest income unless otherwise noted.

Bloomberg 3+ Year Municipal Bond Index is a market-value-weighted index of investment-grade fixed-rate municipal bonds with maturities of three years or more.

Bloomberg Municipal Bond Index is a market value-weighted index of investment-grade municipal bonds with maturities of one year or more.

LIPPER INFORMATION

Lipper Averages are averages of the performance of all mutual funds with their respective investment classification category. The number of funds in each category periodically changes. Lipper, a Refinitiv company, is a nationally recognized organization that ranks the performance of mutual funds.

data.title: Valneva SE: Still Undervalued Travel Vaccine Bet With Robust 2025 Guidance
data.description:
Valneva SE (OTCPK:INRLF) is an OTC ticker that lets US investors invest in a high-potential vaccine company. Their portfolio includes Ixiaro, a Japanese encephalitis vaccine with €94.1 million in sales in 2024. It also has Dukoral, a cholera vaccine primarily used by travelers, that generated €32.3 million in revenue in 2024. Lastly, INRLF has Ixchiq, the world’s first approved chikungunya vaccine, which is now authorized in the US, the EU, and Brazil, a region with endemic chikungunya cases. Similarly, Valneva’s pipeline includes brand extensions for Ixchiq to cover all ages in several areas, as well as VLA15 for Lyme disease, S4V2 for protection against shigellosis, and VLA1601 for Zika virus. Ultimately, I believe Valneva still looks undervalued relative to its peers and long-term prospects, which is why I reiterate my “Buy” rating on them after the recent 22% pullback from their 2025 highs.

Niche Travel Vaccines

Valneva SE is essentially a commercial-stage European biotech firm focused on travel vaccines. It’s an OTC ticker that has lower liquidity than major exchanges. Having said that, Valneva SE is also listed on NASDAQ under the ticker (NASDAQ:VALN) and on the Euronext exchange under VLA. In any case, Valneva SE itself is currently headquartered in Saint-Herblain, France. And I previously covered them back in January 2025 and rated their stock as a “Buy” at the time due to their potential in this vaccine niche.

Moreover, today Valneva's portfolio contains Ixiaro, which is the commercial name used in the US and other jurisdictions. This vaccine is also referred to as “Jespect” in Australia and New Zealand. But basically, Ixiaro helps guard against the Japanese encephalitis [JE] virus, which is a mosquito-borne virus that leads to inflammation of the brain. In fact, Ixiaro is Valneva’s best-selling product, with sales of €94.1 million in FY 2024. This was a 28% YoY revenue growth, which shows how quickly it’s scaling up its potential.

As for its other main vaccine, Dukoral is used to protect against cholera. It is primarily used for travelers to certain regions and provides 2 years of immunization. Dukoral’s FY 2024 revenues amounted to €32.3 million, which was an 8% YoY increase from FY 2023. So, this second product is also an important part of Valneva’s growth strategy, but it’s clear that Ixiaro remains their main growth driver.

Lastly, Valneva’s Ixchiq is the first approved vaccine to prevent the disease caused by the chikungunya virus. It’s intended for people over 18 years old and was initially FDA approved back in November 2023. Shortly after, Valneva launched Ixchiq in 2024 and generated €3.7 million in sales that year. Likewise, Ixchiq is approved for patients 12 years and above in the EU and received marketing authorization in Brazil in April 2024. Interestingly, this was the first marketing authorization in a chikungunya-endemic nation, where more than 150,000 individuals are afflicted with it every year. And fortunately, Ixchiq induces a durable immune response with a single dose, with 96.3% seroresponse at three years.

Valneva’s Broader Pipeline

Having said that, Valneva is also working on label expansion for Ixchiq for all age groups, including adolescents and children, in various markets. Their studies are backed by a Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations [CEPI] grant worth $41.3 million. In fact, their approval in Brazil also allows for a Phase 4 large-scale clinical trial that should give us additional, more robust data on the vaccine's effectiveness. It will also give us a clearer picture of how long Ixchiq actually works as a prophylactic measure against the virus. And indeed, this information will be key for travel regulators to assess the periodicity of immunizations and how long their validity lasts for travelers.

As for Valneva’s second vaccine in its pipeline, this one is currently referred to as “VLA15.” This program is now just a Phase 3 vaccine for Lyme disease. However, Valneva believes it can go ahead with its submission for a Biologics License Application [BLA] and a Marketing Authorization Application as early as 2026. VLA15 could also become an interesting revenue vertical for Valneva because it taps into a relatively sizeable TAM. You see, just Lyme disease testing alone is estimated at $10.9 billion (2023), and rapidly growing at an 8.2% CAGR.

It’s also worth mentioning that Valneva is developing VLA15 in collaboration with Pfizer (PFE). VLA15 is also their most advanced R&D program in clinical development, which has an FDA Fast Track Designation. And management believes that VLA15 could nudge them toward profitability by 2027, as it also includes substantial milestones and royalty payments upon approval. In theory, Valneva’s roadmap targets VLA15’s regulatory submissions in 2026, and first approvals and sales by 2027.

Aside from those main vaccines, Valneva is also working on other investigational vaccines, developed as S4V2 and VLA1601. First, S4V2 is a tetravalent immunization against shigellosis. This condition is a diarrheal illness caused by Shigella bacteria, which is responsible for up to 165 million infections, including 62.3 million in children under five years old. This disease contributes to pediatric morbidity and mortality in many areas of the world, making it a serious condition that should be prevented.

So far, S4V2 is in Phase 2, with Phase 2b results expected in 2025, which could become an important catalyst for the stock later this year. And S4V2’s development is in partnership with LimmaTech Biologics AG. More recently, in April 2025, they announced S4V2’s first vaccination in a pediatric participant for its infant safety and efficacy study.

Secondly, VLA1601 is a vaccine that protects from the Zika virus. This is a mosquito-borne illness associated with birth defects such as microcephaly and other severe brain deficiencies in newborns and Guillain-Barré syndrome [GBS] in adults. However, VLA1601 is in a much earlier R&D stage (Phase 1), with Phase 1b readouts also expected later this year. But given that it’s still a very early-stage IP, I wouldn’t yet consider it an important value driver for now.

Valuation And Risk Analysis

Now, from a valuation perspective, Valneva trades at a $556.6 million market cap. It’s still a very niche stock focused on travel vaccines, but I believe it still has much more potential for the upside. Even though the stock is now 36.7% higher than when I first covered it, they’ve also progressed with other vaccines that could become meaningful revenue contributors in the next few years. Nevertheless, if we look at their balance sheet as of today, Valneva has $174.3 million in cash and short-term investments, against $194.1 million in debt.

Similarly, I estimate they actually turned cash flow positive in Q4 2024 by the tune of $13.7 million. I got this figure by simply adding their cash flows from operations and CAPEX, including intangible asset purchases (in this case, a sale that benefited their cash flow). Still, if we look at their 2024 figures, I believe they burned $86.5 million in negative cash flow for the whole year. That’s probably a more reasonable estimate, which suggests a runway of 2.0 years at ceteris paribus.

Still, note that Valneva has guided to total revenues of €180 to €190 million in 2025, and cutting operating cash burn by over 50% this year as well. So, I think they’re aware that they need to start shifting towards becoming fully self-sustainable now that they have a couple of successful revenue verticals. So, for simplicity, if we assume they reach that $216.6 million revenue target (1.14 USD/EUR exchange rate), it would imply a top-line growth of 12.4% relative to 2024’s total sales of $175.7 million.

In other words, that’s $40.3 million in incremental revenues relative to 2024. And on top of that, their operational cash burn delta reduction of $34.2 million would mean in 2025, Valneva’s cash burn could decline by as much as $74.5 million ($40.3 million + $34.2 million). If that’s the case, their cash burn could be as low as $12.0 million this year ($86.5 million - $74.5 million). And this would extend their runway substantially, so I don’t think they’ll run into any financing issues in the near term.

Plus, it’s important to remember that by 2027, they could start generating additional revenues with VLA15 on Lyme’s disease. This is a substantial market opportunity that could easily be an extra >$1 billion TAM for Valneva. This is why I think that, ultimately, it’s still reasonable to price Valneva through a simple P/S multiple. If we assume their $216.6 million revenue target for 2025, that implies a P/S multiple of 2.6, which, compared to their sector’s median P/S of 3.2, still seems undervalued. And remember, this is a company with a very specific vaccine niche and strong IPs, so their competitive prospects are quite solid in my opinion.

Naturally, the stock is slightly riskier today than when I first covered it because it’s appreciated substantially. And if they fail to reach their revenue targets, or their cash burn doesn’t decline as anticipated, Valneva might need to tap the capital markets for more financing. Given that they’ve already loaded up their balance sheet with debt, I imagine they would probably resort to issuing more equity in the bear case, and that would probably lead to substantial shareholder losses.

But overall, since the stock has pulled back by about 22% from its 2025 highs and seems cheap relative to its peers, I believe those risks are justified at these levels. This is why I reiterate my “Buy” rating on Valneva.

One last note between INRLF and VALN is that both give you direct exposure to Valneva’s equity. The main difference is that VALN is an ADS that represents 2 ordinary shares of Valneva SE. On the other hand, INRLF is an OTC ticker that’s equivalent to 1 ordinary share of Valneva SE. That’s why the VALN trades at basically twice the price of INRLF, but other than that, they give you exposure to Valneva SE’s equity. Still, I would opt for VALN because this security seems to have more trading volume than INRLF, implying less slippage.

Conclusion: Reiterated “Buy”

Overall, I believe Valneva SE is a great way to gain exposure to very specific niche travel vaccines. The business is basically two main vaccines: Ixiaro and Ixchiq. Both of these products are still growing their top lines, and by 2027, I expect Valneva will add VLA15 for Lyme disease. Similarly, management seems to now be focusing on making Valneva a fully self-sustainable operation by cutting down its cash burn this year. And if they also manage to meet their revenue targets in 2025, I think we could soon see Valneva even become profitable in the not-so-distant future. This is why I believe that their valuation multiples still seem disconnected from their underlying successful business. Hence, I reiterate my “Buy” on the shares, either through VALN or INRLF.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

Holdings and characteristics are subject to change. Percentages may be rounded.

Market Overview

US equities registered their first quarterly loss since the third quarter of 2023. Volatility surged and risk sentiment waned as markets grappled with the implications and considerable uncertainty surrounding the Trump administration’s policies on trade, immigration, fiscal spending, tax cuts, and deregulation. Escalating tariffs and the prospect of additional levies in April worsened the outlook for inflation and economic growth, fueling fears of stagflation and recession. Against a turbulent market backdrop, technology indices fell sharply, and growth and small-cap stocks significantly underperformed their value and large-cap counterparts, respectively.

The S&P 500 Index (SPX)(SP500) returned -4.27% over the period. Within the Index, four out of 11 sectors declined for the quarter. Consumer discretionary and information technology were the bottom-performing sectors, while energy and healthcare were the top-performing sectors over the period.

Performance Summary

Hartford Dividend and Growth Fund (I Share) outperformed the S&P 500 Index for the quarter.

Sector allocation, a result of our bottom-up stock-selection process, was a driver of relative outperformance. Allocation effect was driven by our underweight to information technology and consumer discretionary and overweight to energy.

Security selection also contributed to returns. Strong selection in information technology, consumer discretionary and consumer staples was partially offset by selection in communication services, financials, and industrials.

At the issuer level, our top-two relative contributors were not owning NVIDIA and Tesla, while our top-two relative detractors were not owning Berkshire Hathaway and an overweight to FMC.

Positioning & Outlook

We anticipate the potential for heightened volatility will persist amid early indications of economic softening. We continue to believe President Trump’s policy objectives, including tariffs and lower taxes, may prove inflationary, challenging the central bank’s ability to continue lowering rates. Volatility is selectively creating attractive investment opportunities, but the range of outcomes remains wide.

Against this backdrop, we remain focused on companies with consistent pricing power, strong balance sheets, and sustainable and growing cash flows trading at attractive valuations.

At the end of the period, our largest overweights were to healthcare, utilities, and financials. We were most underweight to information technology.

data.title: BST: NAV Growth Supported By Fund's Efficient Strategy
data.description:
Overview

It's common knowledge that Technology companies don't typically prioritize dividends. Instead, they opt to reinvest their earnings back into the business in order to rapidly grow revenue year after year. However, BlackRock Science and Technology Trust (NYSE:BST) uniquely positions itself as a fund that can provide exposure to technology while also putting an emphasis on monthly income. I previously covered BST and issued a buy rating due to attractive valuation and consistent monthly dividends. Since the time of my last coverage, BST has released an updated annual report for 2024 which prompted me to revisit this fund and provide some updated insights.

Looking at the performance over the last twelve months, we can see that BST's price has fallen by about 4.9%. However, most of that decline can be attributed to the uncertainty linked to President Trump's tariff policies. When including all the distributions paid to shareholders during the same time frame, BST maintains a positive total return of a little under 3%. The main appeal of BST is the exposure it provides to tech while also providing a starting dividend yield of 8.8%. Based on the recent reporting, the earnings continue to comfortably cover the distributions.

Following the price decline, BST now trades at an attractive discount to NAV valuation. I believe that long-term investors who are optimistic about the future of technology could benefit from accumulation here. However, the caveat here is that BST only makes sense if you want to specifically put an emphasis on income generation. While the fund has demonstrated long-term consistency and resilience, BST is likely to continue underperforming traditional equities. So let's start by taking a look at the underlying strategies implemented by the fund.

Fund Strategy

According to the most recent fund overview, BST has exposure to 279 different holdings spread throughout different sectors. For instance, Software & Services account for 32.49% of assets. This is followed by exposure to Semiconductors and Tech Hardware, accounting for 27.24% and 12.37% of assets, respectively. The combination of these three sectors makes up over 63% of the fund, so it's safe to assume that these will have the greatest impact on BST's daily price change and total return potential.

Although BST does have some exposure to sectors outside these sub-sectors of technology, it's clear that technology is the main focus here. Therefore, it's possible that BST will experience a similar amount of volatility to the Nasdaq. However, the exposure to technology is essential since BST implements an option strategy to generate additional income from its holdings and support the dividend yield.

According to the fact sheet, BST implements OTM (out-of-the-money) options against its holdings. Options are written against a range of 30% to 40% of the underlying net assets, which I believe to be balanced because it allows the fund to harness volatility and generate income, while simultaneously allowing the fund to participate in capital appreciation during bull markets. The inclusion of an option strategy means that the growth of an asset is capped at whatever the selected strike price is. So if BST has an asset priced at $100 per share, it may write an option against it with a strike price of $110 per share. Any growth above this strike price is sacrificed in exchange for the premium that can be collected.

This is why I anticipate BST to continue underperforming most of its underlying holdings. For instance, we can see that NVIDIA (NVDA) is the largest holding at 12.02% of assets. This is followed by varying exposure to other large-cap companies like Microsoft (MSFT), Apple (AAPL), Broadcom (AVGO), and Meta Platforms (META) just to name a few. The option strategy also allows BST to extract income from equities that don't traditionally pay a dividend, such as Amazon (AMZN).

Just to demonstrate the weakness of the strategy, I pulled a quick total return comparison of BST against its assets over the last three years. We can see that BST is outpaced by each of its top equities by a wide margin. So if you are an investor who seeks to maximize your total return, you would be better off maintaining a position in the underlying holdings instead.

Updated Valuation

Looking back over the last ten years, we can see that BST frequently traded at a premium or very slight discount to its NAV. For instance, BST traded at an average discount to NAV of 1.68% over the last three years. However, BST currently trades at a discount to NAV of 3.7%, making this an attractive time for accumulation. The fund demonstrated resilience during bear markets, while simultaneously proving that it can capitalize on more favorable market conditions. I am optimistic about the future of technology, so I believe that BST could be a great fund for those wanting exposure while putting an emphasis on income generation.

Since the time of my last coverage, BST released an updated annual report for the full year of 2024. Net investment income reported a loss of $0.28 per share, while net realized gains amounted to $8.14 per share. Looking back on prior years, BST has consistently generated losses in net investment income, and I would like to see this drastically improved in the future.

The poor net investment income leaves BST reliant on net realized gains to fuel NAV growth and can certainly be a weakness during market declines. For instance, the fund experienced massive losses in 2022, which BST still hasn't fully recovered from. At least the NAV has shifted to some positive growth from its lows. For instance, here are the year-end NAV amounts:

  • 2020: $51.94 per share
  • 2021: $52.40 per share
  • 2022: $29.11 per share
  • 2023: $34.74 per share
  • 2024: $39.60 per share

The fund has still managed to increase its net assets over time, reinforcing that the strategy is efficient. For instance, net assets were $1.29B back in 2020, but has now increased to $1.38B at the end of 2024. As long as tech continues to thrive, I believe BST's assets will continue to increase.

Outlook & Risk

The most anticipated source of growth in the Technology sector is the growth of AI. So many of BST's holdings are pursuing different AI innovations, products, or services they can push to consumers. Some of the most notable companies that have AI projects in development include NVDA's GPUs, META's AI language model, or MSFT's involvement with ChatGPT. Despite BST's emphasis on income, the fund is still capable of participating in the growth of the market.

Precedence Research estimates that the size of the AI market can increase at a CAGR (compound annual growth rate) of 19.20% through 2034. BST has proven that it can partake in the growth of the tech market and since options are only written against less than half of the portfolio, the fund should be able to see some attractive capital appreciation over time.

However, there are specific risks that investors must be willing to expose themselves to. The emphasis on income means that BST will continue to pay out large distributions during years when the market is declining. As we've established, the fund is reliant on net realized gains to fuel growth and support the distribution. As you can imagine, it's difficult to generate positive net realized gains when markets are in decline, so BST has historically depended on return of capital to make up for the underperformance. Return of capital distributions get pulled directly from the NAV, which contributes to a more dramatic downside.

Just to demonstrate this, I pulled a comparison of BST against a more traditional ETF, Invesco QQQ Trust (QQQ), throughout the year of 2022. Market indices declined throughout 2022 due to the Fed aggressively hiking interest rates to their decade highs in an effort to combat record-breaking inflationary data. QQQ had a total loss of 32.58% for the year, while BST reported a much bigger loss of 38.29% despite having a much larger dividend yield.

Although I am optimistic about the future of the technology sector and BST's ability to participate, there are also some vulnerabilities that can slow growth. Trump did exempt tech from reciprocal tariffs, but there is growing competition in the AI market from outside the US. For instance, we already witnessed how markets negatively reacted when DeepSeek (DEEPSEEK) achieved similar results to ChatGPT at a fraction of the cost. Markets declined as it caused investors to question the value of all the capital that the US has been pouring into the research of AI. Disruption from competitors could cause US confidence to slow and negatively impact BST's ability to provide positive returns.

Dividend Sustainability

As of the latest declared monthly distribution of $0.25 per share, the current dividend yield sits around 8.8%. If we multiply this monthly payout by twelve, we get an estimated annual payout of $3.00 per share. Referring back to the same annual report, BST has had no issues out earning this amount from its underlying strategy. For reference, here are the annual earnings amounts over the last few years:

  • 2020: $21.54 per share
  • 2021: $5.41 per share
  • 2022: ($20.29) per share
  • 2023: $8.63 per share
  • 2024: $7.86 per share

From this perspective, I am confident that the distributions will remain intact for the remainder of 2025 and beyond. Looking back over the last decade, we can see that the distribution has remained fairly consistent. Despite BST offering a high starting yield, the fund has also been able to provide some small dividend raises to shareholders. While I have no expectation of raises going forward, it's nice to know that the fund is able to remain consistent because it enables long-term investors to grow their income through additional contributions and reinvested dividends over time.

However, it's worth noting that most of the distributions have historically been funded by net investment income. When a distribution is funded by net investment income, the dividends are typically classified as ordinary income. Therefore, BST will have tax implications that investors must consider if held outside a tax advantaged account.

Takeaway

In conclusion, I maintain my buy rating on BST as the latest annual report confirms the fund's strength. The NAV continues to trend upward as the fund out earns its distributions. Since the option strategy is only implemented against less than half of the assets, BST is uniquely able to generate income while also participating in bull markets of the technology sector. I am optimistic about the future of tech and AI, and I believe that BST can continue to provide superior income and capital appreciation over time. The distributions remain well-supported, and the consistent track record of dividends means that long-term investors can efficiently compound their income by reinvesting distributions or adding new capital.

data.title: MercadoLibre: Competitive Landscape And Q1 Results
data.description:
Investment Thesis

I recommend buying MercadoLibre (NASDAQ:MELI) shares. This article is a continuation of my initial coverage article published in SA on April 10, 2024, with the same recommendation.

In this article, I will tell investors who have not bought the shares that a compelling opportunity is emerging. Shopee, a division of Sea Limited (NYSE:SE), has been dominating the spotlight and growing a lot.

Many investors are worried about a possible threat to MercadoLibre's dominant position in Brazil, but I do not share this concern. Additionally, we will talk about the good results that the company reported in 1Q25.

Corporate Profile

MercadoLibre is a global e-commerce company founded in 1999 in Argentina. The company currently operates in 18 countries, with the three main contributors being Brazil, Argentina, and Mexico.

In addition to its e-commerce operations, MercadoLibre also has a digital banking operation, known as MercadoPago. At the end of 2024, the e-commerce operation accounted for 59% of total revenues, and the fintech operation accounted for 41%.

Revisiting The Thesis

On April 10, 2024, I published the article in which I first covered MercadoLibre, and since then, the stock has delivered a good return to investors.

At the time, I compared the company to peers such as Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) and Shopee. In this article, I intend to delve deeper into this competitive dynamic, especially in the Brazilian market. But why Brazil? Because the country is the most relevant for the company, as we can see in the revenue segmentation below.

Shopee's Growth

It's not uncommon to see news about Shopee, Sea Limited's e-commerce segment, expanding and opening new distribution centers in Brazil. But what does this mean for the Brazilian e-commerce sector? Data indicates that MercadoLibre is still the leader in market share in Brazil, however, Shopee is already in second place, even surpassing Amazon.

A recent article by Itaú BBA, a segment of Itaú (NYSE:ITUB), brought relevant information about the sector. Shopee has consolidated itself as the second-largest player in Brazil, with a GMV (Gross Merchandise Value) of $10 billion, twice Amazon's GMV and 40% of MercadoLibre's. Estimates indicate that Shopee doubled its GMV in one year.

Given this aggressive growth, many investors fear that Shopee could threaten MercadoLibre's leadership and hegemony in Brazil. I do not believe in this thesis, and I will give my reasons in the next chapter.

Amazon Has Already Tried

We must remember that MercadoLibre began its operations in the 1990s, and the company entered Brazil in the 2000s. Among the largest players, MercadoLibre is more familiar with the economy and the habits of the Brazilian consumer.

Additionally, Amazon entered Brazil in 2012 in a conservative manner. But since then, the company has never managed to threaten MercadoLibre's position, and this is Amazon, one of the magnificent seven. Will Shopee be able to do what Amazon failed to do?

Analyzing The Strategy

Let's dive deeper into the details of the Itaú BBA article. Shopee's average price per item is $7, compared to MercadoLibre's $21. Additionally, the company is trying to increase this average ticket by selling higher-ticket products, such as home appliances. However, we must remember that Brazil deals with interest rates of almost 15% and high household debt. Will this strategy work?

Moreover, 90% of Shopee's GMV is generated by Brazilian sellers, and 33% of these sellers had never sold on any e-commerce site. Is the strategy of serving as a gateway for sellers interesting? I think so at the moment, but I don't see a long-term future here.

MercadoLibre Is Not Just Watching

The company is not just watching Shopee's growth. MercadoLibre recently announced a $6.4 billion investment in Brazil. This is the largest investment announced for the sector and represents an increase in MercadoLibre's investments in Brazil of 48% YoY.

Competition is nothing new for the company. We must remember that MercadoLibre surpassed all local Brazilian players in Brazil during the 2000s and 2010s, and not even Amazon was able to threaten its hegemony.

I don't believe that Shopee will succeed. I'm also not saying that I'm skeptical about Sea Limited's thesis, on the contrary, I just believe that the dominance of Brazilian e-commerce will remain in the hands of MercadoLibre. To enrich our analysis, let's look at the financial analysis of the companies.

Financial Analysis and Valuation

We can see below that the financial analysis is very positive for MercadoLibre, compared to its competitors. The company has the highest revenue growth in the last 5 years, the highest ROE, and has high margins.

Financial metrics come at a price, and the company has the highest PEG among its competitors, but here, I propose a different perspective. Therefore, we will analyze the PEG from the company's own history.

The company is currently trading at the lowest PEG in the last 12 months. If we consider that the PEG of 1.67x returns to the average of 2.3x, we have an upside of 37% for the thesis, which corroborates the buy recommendation.

Latest Earnings Results

MercadoLibre released its 1Q25 results on May 7, and they were very good. As a reference, the company reported net revenue of $5.9 billion (estimate was $5.53 billion) and net income of $494 million (LSEG consensus estimate was $420.9 million).

In the financial segment, MercadoPago's credit portfolio grew 75% year-on-year, and there was a 31% YoY increase in the number of monthly active users, reaching 64 million customers.

In the Commerce segment, Brazil represents about 50% of revenues, and sales in the Brazilian marketplace increased 30% y/y to $5.3 billion. This shows the dominance of the company, and raises the competitive bar for Shopee.

But the big highlight did not come from Brazil, but from Argentina. Argentina was the biggest highlight, with GMV growth of 126% on a neutral exchange rate basis. This Argentine impulse materializes my thesis of April 10, 2024, where I stated that President Milei's policies could make the Argentine operation one of the vectors of growth in the result.

Moreover, the number of items sold increased by 28%, to 492 million, while the total number of unique buyers reached around 67 million. This was the quarter with the highest growth in new users since 2021.

In my opinion, the result was excellent, and the shares rose more than 6% in the post-market. As for the outlook, the company confirms its dominance and shifts all the pressure on its competitors. It is incredible how the company manages to grow so much even in adverse scenarios.

Potential Threats To The Bullish Thesis

To talk about risks, we need to talk a little about the latest earnings. Investments and growth in the credit segment have had an effect on profitability as the company deals with competition.

As we can see above, the gross margin did not grow, and the growth of only 8.3% y/y in net income does not seem to justify MercadoLibre's high valuation.

The high valuation level can be confirmed by the F valuation rating below. Additionally, it is important to note that SA's powerful Quant tools recommend that investors hold their shares, which differs from my buy recommendation.

The Bottom Line

The aggressive growth of Shopee, a segment of Sea Limited, in the Brazilian market has caught the attention of investors worldwide. Some believe that the company will soon overtake MercadoLibre, I disagree.

MercadoLibre is accustomed to competition, the company has long known the habits of Brazilian consumers. It is no wonder that not even Amazon has been able to threaten the company's hegemony in Brazilian e-commerce.

The results released in Q1 were very good, showing the strength of the Brazilian Commerce operation and the potential that the Argentine operations have to add to the business. The outlook is very positive.

Based on this analysis, I recommend buying MercadoLibre shares. In my view, this entire competitive context has turned into an extra discount for MercadoLibre shares, which makes me optimistic about the thesis.
